text.div:
Generated Narrative: ImplementationGuide dhis2.cancer.registry

DHIS2CancerRegistryIG

The official URL for this implementation guide is: 

https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/ImplementationGuide/dhis2.cancer.registry

This implementation guide defines the FHIR logical models and terminology for the DHIS2 Cancer Registry metadata package, aligned with CanReg5 data standards developed by the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC). The FHIR artifacts in this IG are generated from a DHIS2 Tracker program for individual-level cancer case registration supporting population-based monitoring, with embedded data quality control checks based on CanReg5 validation logic covering age, sex, topography, morphology, behaviour, grade and basis of diagnosis combinations.

name: DHIS2CancerRegistryIG
title: DHIS2 Cancer Registry Implementation Guide
description: This implementation guide defines the FHIR logical models and terminology for the DHIS2 Cancer Registry metadata package, aligned with CanReg5 data standards developed by the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC). The FHIR artifacts in this IG are generated from a DHIS2 Tracker program for individual-level cancer case registration supporting population-based monitoring, with embedded data quality control checks based on CanReg5 validation logic covering age, sex, topography, morphology, behaviour, grade and basis of diagnosis combinations.

extension.valueMarkdown: Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on HL7 Terminology
Automatically added as a dependency - all IGs depend on the HL7 Extension Pack

resource.name: Address
resource.description: Administrative address code of the patient at incidence. Country-specific — must be localised. Corresponds to the CR - Address data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Age at Incidence
resource.description: Age of the patient at the date of incidence. Corresponds to the CR - Age data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Basis of Diagnosis
resource.description: How the cancer diagnosis was established, coded according to the ICD-O-3 basis of diagnosis classification (codes 0–9). Corresponds to the CR - Basis diagnosis data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Behaviour
resource.description: ICD-O-3 behaviour code of the neoplasm: 0=benign, 1=uncertain, 2=in situ, 3=malignant (primary site). Corresponds to the CR - Behaviour data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Cancer Registration EpisodeOfCare
resource.description: Episode of Care representing the overall enrollment of a patient in the cancer registry. Corresponds to the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program enrollment. All stage-level Encounters (Tumor, Source, Follow-up) reference this episode.

resource.name: Cancer Registry
resource.description: Enrollment of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. Captures core patient demographic information required to register an individual in the cancer registry, aligned with the IARC standard data requirements for population-based cancer registration. The tracked entity type is 'Person'. The Patient ID is automatically generated at enrollment using the pattern CURRENT_DATE(yyyy)+SEQUENTIAL(####), following the CanReg5 convention.

resource.name: Cancer Registry Encounter Types
resource.description: Encounter types representing the three repeatable program stages of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program.

resource.name: Cancer Registry Encounter Types Value Set
resource.description: Value set of encounter types for cancer registration stages.

resource.name: Cancer Registry Observation Codes
resource.description: Code system defining observation type codes for cancer registry observations. Used where no standard LOINC code exists for cancer-registry-specific concepts.

resource.name: Cancer Registry Observation Codes Value Set
resource.description: Value set containing all cancer-registry-specific observation type codes.

resource.name: Cancer Registry Patient
resource.description: Minimum patient profile for CanReg5-aligned population-based cancer registration. Requires a patient registration number (REGNO) of at least 8 characters following the CanReg5 CURRENT_DATE(yyyy)+SEQUENTIAL(####) convention, family name, administrative sex, and date of birth.

resource.name: CR - Address
resource.description: Address codes containing placeholder values that must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the administrative geography of the country or region. The recommended approach uses text-type data elements combined with dependent dropdown lists, aligning with the CanReg5 convention where the address variable is two characters in length encoding a combination of two administrative levels.

resource.name: CR - Address value set
resource.description: Value set of address codes containing placeholder values that must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the administrative geography of the country or region.

resource.name: CR - Basis diagnosis
resource.description: Basis of diagnosis codes aligned with the CanReg5 basis dictionary. Codes 0-4 represent non-microscopically confirmed diagnoses (death certificate only, clinical, clinical investigation/ultrasound, surgery/autopsy, laboratory test). Codes 5-8 represent microscopically confirmed diagnoses (cytology, histology of metastasis, histology of primary, autopsy with histology). Code 9 represents unknown basis. Used in the Basis data quality check.

resource.name: CR - Basis diagnosis value set
resource.description: Value set of basis of diagnosis codes aligned with the CanReg5 basis dictionary. Codes 0-4 are non-microscopically confirmed, codes 5-8 are microscopically confirmed, and code 9 is unknown.

resource.name: CR - Behaviour
resource.description: Tumour behaviour codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 behaviour dictionary. Used in the Tumor stage to classify the biological behaviour of the neoplasm. These codes are referenced in data quality checks including the Topography-Behaviour and Sex-Morphology checks. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards.

resource.name: CR - Behaviour value set
resource.description: Value set of tumour behaviour codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 behaviour dictionary, used in the Tumor stage to classify the biological behaviour of the neoplasm.

resource.name: CR - Grade
resource.description: Tumour grade and differentiation codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 grade dictionary. Codes 1-4 represent histological grade (well differentiated to undifferentiated/anaplastic). Codes 5-8 are reserved for lymphomas and leukaemias (T-cell, B-cell, Null cell, NK cell). Code 9 represents unknown/not stated. Grade is mandatory only when behaviour is malignant (3). These codes are used in the Grade data quality check.

resource.name: CR - Grade value set
resource.description: Value set of tumour grade and differentiation codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 grade dictionary. Includes histological grades 1-4, lymphoma/leukaemia-specific codes 5-8, and unknown (9). Grade is mandatory only when behaviour is malignant (3).

resource.name: CR - Morphology Family
resource.description: Morphological family grouping codes derived from the CanReg5 MorphFam.txt lookup file. Each morphology code is assigned to a morphological family, which is used in the Sex-Morphology check (to identify rare sex-morphology combinations) and the Topography-Morphology check (to evaluate morphology-topography combinations against MUST and MUST-NOT lookup tables). Code 99 (N/A) indicates the family is accepted with any topography.

resource.name: CR - Morphology Family value set
resource.description: Value set of morphological family grouping codes derived from the CanReg5 MorphFam.txt lookup file, used in the Sex-Morphology and Topography-Morphology data quality checks.

resource.name: CR - Morphology Group
resource.description: Morphology group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. Groups are derived from morphology codes using the mapping logic defined in the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester class. Groups 8-14 represent haematopoietic/lymphoid tissue subtypes (systemic groups). Group 0 indicates an invalid morphology, and group 17 indicates an unspecified morphology.

resource.name: CR - Morphology Group value set
resource.description: Value set of morphology group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check, derived from the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester mapping logic.

resource.name: CR - Morphology result
resource.description: Intermediate morphology evaluation result codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester check. When the morphology comparison between two tumours is inconclusive (undecided), the check proceeds to topography evaluation. 'Invalid' indicates one of the morphology groups is invalid (0). 'Go to topography' indicates the morphology evaluation was undecided and topography comparison is needed.

resource.name: CR - Morphology result value set
resource.description: Value set of intermediate morphology evaluation result codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester check to determine whether to proceed to topography evaluation.

resource.name: CR - Morphology4
resource.description: ICD-O-3.2 morphology codes (first four digits) mapped from the CanReg5 morphology dictionary (morphology4.tsv). These codes represent the histological type of the neoplasm. Since the field is configured as a closed option set, validity of morphology values is guaranteed by the data entry interface. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards and used in multiple data quality checks.

resource.name: CR - Morphology4 value set
resource.description: Value set of ICD-O-3.2 morphology codes (first four digits) mapped from the CanReg5 morphology dictionary, representing histological types of neoplasms.

resource.name: CR - Multiple primary test
resource.description: Result codes for the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. This check determines whether two or more tumours registered for the same patient represent duplicate registrations of the same tumour or distinct multiple primary tumours, based on the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester logic.

resource.name: CR - Multiple primary test value set
resource.description: Value set of result codes for the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check, determining whether tumours are duplicate registrations, distinct multiple primaries, or have unknown topography.

resource.name: CR - Placeholder: Source type
resource.description: Placeholder source type codes for the Source stage. These values must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the source types relevant to the local registry context (e.g. hospital records, pathology laboratory reports, death certificates).

resource.name: CR - Placeholder: Source type value set
resource.description: Value set of placeholder source type codes for the Source stage. These values must be customised prior to implementation to reflect local source types (e.g. hospital records, pathology reports, death certificates).

resource.name: CR - Sex
resource.description: Sex codes aligned with the CanReg5 sex dictionary, using numeric codes. These codes are referenced in multiple data quality checks throughout the program, including the Sex-Topography and Sex-Morphology checks. Any modification to these codes or substitution with a differently coded option set would break the logic of those checks.

resource.name: CR - Sex value set
resource.description: Value set of sex codes aligned with the CanReg5 sex dictionary. These numeric codes are referenced in multiple data quality checks including Sex-Topography and Sex-Morphology checks.

resource.name: CR - Site
resource.description: Anatomical site codes used to filter topography values in a dependent dropdown list. When a site is selected, only the topography codes valid for the chosen site are presented for selection, simplifying data entry for registry staff.

resource.name: CR - Site value set
resource.description: Value set of anatomical site codes used to filter topography values in a dependent dropdown list for simplified data entry.

resource.name: CR - Topography
resource.description: ICD-O-3 topography codes mapped from the CanReg5 topography dictionary (topography.tsv). These codes represent the anatomical site of the tumour origin. The topography field is implemented as a dependent dropdown list filtered by the selected Site. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards and used in multiple data quality checks.

resource.name: CR - Topography Group
resource.description: Topography group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. Groups are derived from topography codes using the mapping logic defined in the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester class. Group 80 indicates an unspecified topography. These groups determine whether tumours at different sites should be considered duplicate or multiple primaries.

resource.name: CR - Topography Group value set
resource.description: Value set of topography group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check, derived from the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester mapping logic.

resource.name: CR - Topography Morphology key
resource.description: Keys used in the Topography-Morphology data quality check to determine how each morphological family relates to topography. NA (*): the family is accepted with any topography. Plus (+): the family has a restricted set of expected topography sites checked against the MUST list. Minus (-): the family has a restricted set of prohibited topography sites checked against the MUST-NOT list.

resource.name: CR - Topography Morphology key value set
resource.description: Value set of keys used in the Topography-Morphology data quality check. NA (*) means accepted with any topography; Plus (+) means checked against the MUST list; Minus (-) means checked against the MUST-NOT list.

resource.name: CR - Topography value set
resource.description: Value set of ICD-O-3 topography codes mapped from the CanReg5 topography dictionary, representing anatomical sites of tumour origin.

resource.name: CR - Tumor number
resource.description: Tumor number codes used as a unique identifier for each tumor within the registry. Combined with the Patient ID, the Tumor Number constitutes the Tumor ID (the composite identifier that uniquely identifies a tumor record). A validation mechanism prevents the same tumor number from being assigned to more than one tumor belonging to the same patient.

resource.name: CR - Tumor number value set
resource.description: Value set of tumor number identifiers. Combined with the Patient ID, the Tumor Number forms the Tumor ID composite identifier that uniquely identifies a tumor record within the system.

resource.name: CR - Vital status
resource.description: Vital status codes used in the Follow-up stage to record the current status of the registered patient. When the status is 'Died', the date of death must also be recorded.

resource.name: CR - Vital status value set
resource.description: Value set of vital status codes used in the Follow-up stage to record the current status of the registered patient.

resource.name: Date of Death
resource.description: Date of death of the cancer registry patient, recorded when the vital status is 'dead'. Corresponds to the CR - Date of death data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Follow-up stage.

resource.name: DHIS2 Data Elements
resource.description: Code system for 47 unique DHIS2 data elements from all selected programs

resource.name: DHIS2 Tracked Entity Attributes
resource.description: Code system for 5 unique DHIS2 tracked entity attributes

resource.name: Example Age at Incidence
resource.description: Age at incidence: 54 years.

resource.name: Example Basis of Diagnosis
resource.description: Basis of diagnosis observation: 7 — Histology of primary.

resource.name: Example Behaviour
resource.description: Behaviour observation: 3 — Malignant, primary site.

resource.name: Example Cancer Registration Episode
resource.description: Episode of care representing the patient's enrollment into the Cancer Registry programme.

resource.name: Example Cancer Registry Patient
resource.description: A 54-year-old female patient enrolled in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry.

resource.name: Example Follow-up Encounter
resource.description: Encounter recording a 6-month follow-up contact.

resource.name: Example Grade
resource.description: Histological grade observation: Grade 2 — moderately differentiated.

resource.name: Example Incidence Date
resource.description: Incidence date observation: 2026-01-01.

resource.name: Example Morphology
resource.description: Morphology observation: 8500 — Infiltrating duct carcinoma, NOS.

resource.name: Example Site
resource.description: Site observation: Breast.

resource.name: Example Source Encounter
resource.description: Encounter recording the Source stage event: hospital notification.

resource.name: Example Source Tumor Number
resource.description: Source links to tumor number 01.

resource.name: Example Source Type
resource.description: Source type: placeholder source type (to be localised).

resource.name: Example Topography
resource.description: Topography observation: C50.4 — Upper-outer quadrant of breast.

resource.name: Example Tumor Encounter
resource.description: Encounter recording the Tumor stage event for primary breast carcinoma.

resource.name: Example Tumor Number
resource.description: Tumour number: 01 (first tumour registered for this patient).

resource.name: Example Vital Status
resource.description: Patient alive at 6-month follow-up.

resource.name: Follow up
resource.description: Follow-up stage of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. This repeatable stage records the follow-up status of the registered patient over time, capturing the date of last contact, the vital status, and, in cases where the status is death, the date of death. This stage is not associated with a specific tumor or source.

resource.name: Follow up Questionnaire
resource.description: Questionnaire for the Follow-up stage of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. Records the follow-up status of registered patients over time, capturing the date of last contact, vital status, and date of death when applicable. This stage is not associated with a specific tumor or source.

resource.name: Follow-up Encounter
resource.description: Encounter representing a single Follow-up stage event in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. Each follow-up contact with a registered patient to ascertain vital status is recorded as a separate encounter. Observations within this encounter capture the vital status, date of last contact, and optional date of death.

resource.name: Histological Grade
resource.description: Histological grade (differentiation) of the tumour as defined in ICD-O-3. LOINC 33732-9. Grade codes: 1=well differentiated, 2=moderately differentiated, 3=poorly differentiated, 4=undifferentiated, 5–8=lymphomas/leukaemias, 9=not determined. Corresponds to the CR - Grade data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Incidence Date
resource.description: Date of incidence (diagnosis date) of the cancer case. Corresponds to the CR - Incidence date data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Morphology
resource.description: ICD-O-3 morphology code (4-digit histological type) of the neoplasm, e.g. 8500 = infiltrating duct carcinoma. Corresponds to the CR - Morphology data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Rare Tumor Classification
resource.description: Indicates whether the registered tumor has been classified as rare according to the IARC/CanReg5 data quality checks. This is a computed flag derived from the topography-morphology combination evaluated against the IARC rare cancer reference tables. Corresponds to the CR - Rare data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Site
resource.description: Higher-level anatomical site grouping derived from the ICD-O-3 topography. Corresponds to the CR - Site data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Source
resource.description: Source stage of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. This repeatable stage records the documentation sources from which a cancer case has been notified to the registry. Each source represents a piece of documentation, such as a hospital record, pathology report, or death certificate, that supports the registration of a specific tumor. Sources are linked to a specific tumor via the Tumor Number.

resource.name: Source Notification Encounter
resource.description: Encounter representing a single Source stage event in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. Each documentation source (hospital record, pathology report, death certificate, etc.) that notifies the registry about a cancer case is recorded as a separate encounter. Observations within this encounter capture the source type and linked tumor number.

resource.name: Source Questionnaire
resource.description: Questionnaire for the Source stage of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. Records the documentation sources from which a cancer case has been notified to the registry. Each source represents a piece of documentation (such as a hospital record, pathology report, or death certificate) linked to a specific tumor via the Tumor Number. Contains a visible Source section and a hidden TUMOURIDSOURCETABLE section used for CanReg5 data extraction.

resource.name: Source Tumor Number
resource.description: Tumor number that this source notification relates to. Links the source to a specific tumor within the patient's registration. Corresponds to the CR - Source: Tumor number data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Source stage.

resource.name: Source Type
resource.description: Type of documentation source that notified the cancer registry about a case (e.g. hospital, laboratory, death certificate). Country-specific — must be localised. Corresponds to the CR - Source Type data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Source stage.

resource.name: Topography
resource.description: ICD-O-3 topography code indicating the anatomical site of the primary tumour. Corresponds to the CR - Topography data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Transform: Cancer Registry Enrollment to EpisodeOfCare
resource.description: Maps DHIS2 Cancer Registry enrollment to a FHIR EpisodeOfCare resource.

resource.name: Transform: Cancer Registry Enrollment to Patient
resource.description: Maps DHIS2 Cancer Registry enrollment TEAs to a FHIR Patient resource.

resource.name: Transform: Follow-up Stage to Encounter + Observations
resource.description: Maps a DHIS2 Follow-up stage event to a FHIR Encounter and Observation bundle.

resource.name: Transform: Source Stage to Encounter + Observations
resource.description: Maps a DHIS2 Source stage event to a FHIR Encounter and Observation bundle.

resource.name: Transform: Tumor Stage to Encounter + Observations
resource.description: Maps a DHIS2 Tumor stage event to a FHIR Encounter and Observation bundle.

resource.name: Tumor
resource.description: Tumor stage of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. This repeatable stage is the central component capturing all key clinical and epidemiological information related to an individual cancer case.

resource.name: Tumor Number
resource.description: Sequential tumor number within the patient's registration (1–10). Corresponds to the CR - Tumor number data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Tumor stage.

resource.name: Tumor Questionnaire
resource.description: Questionnaire for the Tumor stage of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. This is the central data collection component, structured into sections: Patient (demographics and geographic information), Tumor (core variables aligned with CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 standards including site, topography, morphology, behaviour, basis of diagnosis, and grade), Check Status (triggers for executing data quality checks), Checks (hidden section storing individual check results for rare/invalid classifications), Morphology Topography Check (hidden section for topography-morphology validation), Multiple Primary Tester (hidden section for duplicate/multiple primary determination), Rare Status (confirmation of flagged rare cases by authorised supervisors), and Tumor ID (hidden section supporting CanReg5 data extraction).

resource.name: Tumor Registration Encounter
resource.description: Encounter representing a single Tumor stage event in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program. Each time a tumour is registered for a patient, a new Encounter of this type is created. Observations for each data element (topography, morphology, behaviour, grade, basis of diagnosis, etc.) reference this Encounter.

resource.name: Vital Status
resource.description: Follow-up vital status of the registered cancer patient (alive, dead, or unknown). Corresponds to the CR - Status data element in the DHIS2 Cancer Registry Follow-up stage.
